name: CYTOMEGALOVIRUS INFECTION
definition: double-stranded DNA virus with replication inside cell nucleus causing a lytic productive /

demographics.Incidence: 0.4-2.4% of liveborn infants; 40,000 babies born each year with CMV infection in

general: cortical + subcortical areas of hypointensity on T1WI + hyperintensity on T2WI
enhancing mass lesions (only with advanced AIDS)
thick and chunky postinflammatory periventricular Ca2+
faint punctate Ca2+ in basal ganglia + thalami + parenchyma
expansion of CSF spaces in posterior fossa
severe diffuse hypoplasia / dysplasia of cerebellum
lissencephaly = smooth surface with absence of sulcation of a thin / thickened cortical
diffuse / focal polymicrogyria = nodular cortical surface + irregular scalloped gray-
white matter disease (in up to 22%) = leukoencephalopathy = static nonprogressive focal
periventricular cysts = subependymal cysts (= focal areas of necrosis + glial reaction)
ventricular adhesions = intraventricular septa = thin strands of tissue crossing the ventricle
lenticulostriate vasculopathy (in up to 27%) = uni- / bilateral curvilinear highly echogenic

clinical.Rx: no effective treatment for maternal infection
clinical.Prognosis: poor neurologic outcome with microcephaly + calcifications
